g.text: Per-instance writes → packed vec4s in one interleaved VBO → bucketed dirty ranges → one drawIndexed per material
→ branchless effect gating in the fragment shader
1. Assignment funnel — Sprite → Run → SpriteBatch (1 draw call)
Sort order: batches sorted by (layer, batchIdx); within a batch, instances sorted by zIndex (batchSortSystem, in-place swapSlots).
2. Per-instance buffer layout — 4 views, 1 interleaved buffer
instanceUV offset 0..3 uv.x uv.y uv.w uv.h
instanceColor offset 4..7 r g b a ← free-slot = a=0
instanceSystem offset 8..11 flipX flipY sysFlags ENABLE-BITS ★
instanceExtras offset 12..15 shadowR res0 res1 res2
effectBuf0 effectBuf1 effectBuf2 3 × vec4 = 12 floats / slot (MAX = 12)
O(1), no ECS subscriber, no archetype move.
O(N slots) once per frame to flush.
Each field gets a sequential float offset.
Buffer (16,384 slots) split into 64 buckets × 256 slots:
markDirty(slot): b = slot >>> 8 → bucketState[b] = min, bucketLastSlot[b] = max (~5 ns, no allocs)
dirtyBuckets ≥ threshold → clearUpdateRanges() + needsUpdate → three picks bufferData fast-path (1 upload)
else → one addUpdateRange(first*stride, (last-first+1)*stride) per dirty bucket
Thresholds: matrix = 5 interleaved = 3 custom (effectBuf) = 3 Bucket = 256 (power of 2)
4. Effect bits → shader mix — toggle effects, no pipeline rebuild
instanceSystem.w (one float per slot, treated as a bitmask):
// 1 float write into the interleaved buffer. No rebuilds.
for (const { effectClass, bitIndex, attrs } of effectData) {
const effectResult = effectClass._node({ inputColor: color, inputUV: atlasUV, attrs })
color = mix(color, effectResult, enabled) // branchless gate
Same compiled shader runs for every sprite in the batch. enabled = 0 → mix returns base color; enabled = 1 → mix returns effect.
All effects are still evaluated, but their contribution is gated by a single MAD — no dynamic branching, no per-instance pipeline switch.
5. Per-frame lifecycle — CPU produces, GPU consumes
~5 ns · zero allocs · O(1) direct buffer poke
— no per-frame Sets, no cross-group bleed.
Only the dirty regions cross the bus.
Most frames touch < 5 % of the buffer.
no map/unmap, no staging copy on the hot path.
VS: gl_Position = projection × view × instanceMatrix × cornerPos
FS: baseColor (atlas sample + UV flip + tint)
for each effect: color = mix(color, eff_i, bit_i)
Two ordering paths on the GPU side:
▸ Alpha-blend (default) depthWrite = false → CPU sort by zIndex (batchSortSystem, in-place swapSlots)
▸ alphaTest > 0 depthWrite = true → GPU depth test orders fragments; CPU sort skipped
Result: one drawIndexed per batch, one pipeline per material, per-instance state in 6 buffer fetches.
